sections.title: When using the right-click Join-Crew option

lines.speaker: Fancy Dan
lines.text: I don't let anyone on the team if they are wearing a hat or a helmet.

lines.speaker: Fancy Dan
lines.text: You can go and store it in the deposit box next to Jimmy's stall.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why not?

lines.speaker: Fancy Dan
lines.text: Well, in order to tell my crew from San Fan's I will need to give you a hat like mine.

lines.speaker: Fancy Dan
lines.text: If you are wearing a hat it might get lost when I give you my crew's hat.

lines.speaker: Fancy Dan
lines.text: You wouldn't want that would you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, that sounds very reasonable in fact.

lines.speaker: Fancy Dan
lines.text: I'll be glad to have you aboard, but you will need to go and store all of your gear first.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you mean?

lines.speaker: Fancy Dan
lines.text: Well, you will need all of your inventory space for the ingredients, and all of the other things I'll need you to haul about.

lines.speaker: Fancy Dan
lines.text: There is a bank deposit box next to Jimmy's stall if you need it. Come and see me when you have a fully empty inventory.

lines.speaker: Fancy Dan
lines.text: Sadly for you I have more than enough people on my crew.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, all right.

lines.speaker: Fancy Dan
lines.text: Is that... thing yours?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You mean my cat?

lines.speaker: Fancy Dan
lines.text: Yes, I mean your cat! I HATE cats! Get it out of here!

lines.speaker: Fancy Dan
lines.text: Filthy things, no better than the rats that they catch.

lines.speaker: Fancy Dan
lines.text: Arr!

sections.title: When the player has less than 40 cooking

lines.speaker: Fancy Dan
lines.text: So, how well can you cook?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, I can, err, cook a bit...

lines.speaker: Fancy Dan
lines.text: Well 'a bit' doesn't cut it in here. Come back when you have a cooking level of 40.

lines.speaker: Fancy Dan
lines.text: Arr!

lines.speaker: Fancy Dan
lines.text: Is there something I can help ye with?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I would like to join your crew.

lines.speaker: Fancy Dan
lines.text: Of course you would!

lines.speaker: Fancy Dan
lines.text: However, I don't let anyone on the team if they are wearing a hat or a helmet.

lines.speaker: Fancy Dan
lines.text: You can go and store it in the deposit box next to Jimmy's stall.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why not?

lines.speaker: Fancy Dan
lines.text: Well, in order to tell my crew from San Fan's I will need to give you a hat like mine.

lines.speaker: Fancy Dan
lines.text: If you are wearing a hat it might get lost when I give you my crew's hat.

lines.speaker: Fancy Dan
lines.text: You wouldn't want that would you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, that sounds very reasonable in fact.

lines.speaker: Fancy Dan
lines.text: Well, I'll be glad to have you aboard, but you will need to go and store all of your gear first.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you mean?

lines.speaker: Fancy Dan
lines.text: Well, you will need all of your inventory space for the ingredients, and all of the other things I'll need you to haul about.

lines.speaker: Fancy Dan
lines.text: There is a bank deposit box next to Jimmy's stall if you need it. Come and see me when you have a fully empty inventory.

lines.speaker: Fancy Dan
lines.text: Sadly for you however, I have more than enough people on my crew.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, all right.

lines.speaker: Fancy Dan
lines.text: Is that...thing yours?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You mean my cat?

lines.speaker: Fancy Dan
lines.text: Yes, I mean your cat! I HATE cats! Get it out of here!

lines.speaker: Fancy Dan
lines.text: Filthy things, no better than the rats that they catch.

lines.speaker: Fancy Dan
lines.text: Arr, well we'll get you sorted out with a team hat right away.

lines.speaker: Fancy Dan
lines.text: Go wait in the waiting room, I'll call you when we are ready to begin.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Who are you, and what is this place?

lines.speaker: Fancy Dan
lines.text: Arrharrharr!

lines.speaker: Fancy Dan
lines.text: Do you not recognise me? I am the famous dandy pirate 'Fancy' Dan!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, of course, any pirate knows who you are.

lines.speaker: Fancy Dan
lines.text: But of course.

lines.speaker: Fancy Dan
lines.text: As for what this place is, it is my glorious still, producing the best quality 'rum' this side of Braindeath Island.

lines.speaker: Fancy Dan
lines.text: Oh, and the run-down swill-producing ditch that San Fan over there calls a still.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So with two stills you have plenty of 'rum' right?

lines.speaker: Fancy Dan
lines.text: Well we would do, if that hatchet-faced harridan wouldn't keep trying to torch the place!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I say!

lines.speaker: Fancy Dan
lines.text: A little while ago we had some real problems getting rum to the island, hence the two stills.

lines.speaker: Fancy Dan
lines.text: However, after a little misunderstanding involving San's still and a barrel of gunpowder our relationship seems to have become a little strained.

lines.speaker: Fancy Dan
lines.text: Apparently it is perfectly within the pirate code to burn each others stills, so long as nobody directly attacks the other person's crew, so rum production has taken a slight dip of late.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I help out?

lines.speaker: Fancy Dan
lines.text: Yes, probably. I have a bunch of jobs that need doing, if you feel up to it.

lines.speaker: Fancy Dan
lines.text: What would you like to know about?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How do I help you brew 'Rum'?

lines.speaker: Fancy Dan
lines.text: Well the stills we have set up are based on the ones on Braindeath island... well roughly at any rate.

lines.speaker: Fancy Dan
lines.text: All you have to do is help put the ingredients into the hoppers and keep the boilers burning.

lines.speaker: Fancy Dan
lines.text: If you add enough ingredients to the hoppers then you have the ability to pick up a single bottle of rum when it brews.

lines.speaker: Fancy Dan
lines.text: So, if ye add 5 buckets of water to the hoppers, then ye get access to one bottle. If you add 3 bowls of coloured water you get access to one bottle, and if you add one of anything else you get access to one bottle.

lines.speaker: Fancy Dan
lines.text: This is cumulative, meaning that if you add (for example) 10 buckets of water, 3 sweetgrubs and 6 bowls of coloured water, then you have a chance to collect 7 bottles of rum for the team.

lines.speaker: Fancy Dan
lines.text: The boilers are easy as pie to sort out. You just put some logs in, light them with a tinderbox, and then keep putting logs in until they are good and hot.

lines.speaker: Fancy Dan
lines.text: Three logs should do it, but it's best to keep heaping them in just in case.

lines.speaker: Fancy Dan
lines.text: The ingredients are; Bitternuts, Scrapey Tree Bark, Sweetgrubs, Buckets of Water and some Coloured Water.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where can I get Bitternuts?

lines.speaker: Fancy Dan
lines.text: There are plenty of Bitternut trees inside this walled off area. The problem is that the trees are too tricky for a human to climb.

lines.speaker: Fancy Dan
lines.text: You will need to catch a monkey and send it up the tree to get the nut for you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Send it up the tree? How does that work?

lines.speaker: Fancy Dan
lines.text: Well, if you were a monkey, and some random person grabbed you, dumped some dye in your fur and shoved you up a tree, you would hurl the closest heavy object at them too.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Wait a second, dye?

lines.speaker: Fancy Dan
lines.text: Yes, you'll need to get a bowl of coloured water and pour it on the monkey. Otherwise you won't be able to tell which team the monkey belongs too.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That makes sense...I think...

lines.speaker: Fancy Dan
lines.text: Anything else?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I ask about something else?

lines.speaker: Fancy Dan
lines.text: Ok, what else?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So...how would I go about sabotaging the enemy still?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where can I get Scrapey tree bark...and why do I get the feeling I won't like the answer?

lines.speaker: Fancy Dan
lines.text: Inside this walled area there are a few Scrapey trees. They are the big, ill looking ones, you can't miss them.

lines.speaker: Fancy Dan
lines.text: Basically, you cut them down and then carve the bark off with a knife.

lines.speaker: Fancy Dan
lines.text: It's pretty simple, except the sap tends so make people feel kind off ill if they get any on themselves.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How ill?

lines.speaker: Fancy Dan
lines.text: Don't worry, we aren't talking deadly ill or anything.

lines.speaker: Fancy Dan
lines.text: It's more like you'll feel quite dizzy and weak for a bit.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Joy...

lines.speaker: Fancy Dan
lines.text: Anything else?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sweetgrubs? What are they?

lines.speaker: Fancy Dan
lines.text: Well, lacking any sluglings in the area, we add these little grubs called Sweetgrubs. If you ignore the gag- inducing aftertaste, they are quite sweet.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How... how can you drink this stuff?

lines.speaker: Fancy Dan
lines.text: Quite easily!

lines.speaker: Fancy Dan
lines.text: Pirates are renowned as being tough for a reason.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So where do I get these grubs from anyway?

lines.speaker: Fancy Dan
lines.text: Well, there are a few sweetgrub mounds inside there. We have a handful of rat meat that you can use on the mounds to draw the grubs to the surface.

lines.speaker: Fancy Dan
lines.text: Mind your fingers though, they are voracious little things.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And here was me thinking that nothing could be more disgusting than Braindeath 'Rum'.

lines.speaker: Captian Braindeath
lines.text: Arr, that's where ye'd be wrong [lad/lass], 'cos new, improved Braindeath 'Rum' Classic is now 200% more disgustin' than any leadin' competitor!

lines.speaker: Captian Braindeath
lines.text: Legal disclaimer; the dangerous amount of disgustin' in new Braindeath 'Rum' Classic be too much for pregnant women, donkeys and anyone with more than seven letters in their last name.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What was that?

lines.speaker: Fancy Dan
lines.text: What was what?

lines.speaker: Fancy Dan
lines.text: Is there anything else?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How do I get...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Wait, no, that question makes me sound like I haven't a clue what I'm doing.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where can I get buckets in there?

lines.speaker: Fancy Dan
lines.text: There are a few lying about. Help yourself.

lines.speaker: Fancy Dan
lines.text: There is a pump in the still, so long as it's working you should have almost unlimited water.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hold on, do I have to add anything disgusting and/or potential lethal to the water before I pour it in?

lines.speaker: Fancy Dan
lines.text: Perish the thought!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok, just checking.

lines.speaker: Fancy Dan
lines.text: Anything else?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What sort of coloured water are we talking about here?

lines.speaker: Fancy Dan
lines.text: Well, the other still has an area of waste ground with a flower growing on it. If you go and grab one of the flowers and jam it in the kettle, the water will boil out the colour.

lines.speaker: Fancy Dan
lines.text: We use the colour to keep our rum easily identifiable.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So I just empty the kettle into the hopper, right?

lines.speaker: Fancy Dan
lines.text: No, no, you'll need to take an empty bowl and fill it from the kettle.

lines.speaker: Fancy Dan
lines.text: That way you can use the stuff on a monkey if you need to.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Use it on a monkey...of course.

lines.speaker: Fancy Dan
lines.text: Anything else?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How do I repair the still?

lines.speaker: Fancy Dan
lines.text: Well, assuming those scurvy dogs have managed to set any of it on fire, then you'll need to tip a bucket of water on it before you can repair it.

lines.speaker: Fancy Dan
lines.text: Then, it all comes down to what part of it was damaged.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How can I repair hoppers?

lines.speaker: Fancy Dan
lines.text: Well, assuming that they are not on fire, all you need to do is use the heavy lumber patches. They are big enough to cover most holes.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Will I need a hammer?

lines.speaker: Fancy Dan
lines.text: No, just shove it into place and whack it until it lines up.

lines.speaker: Fancy Dan
lines.text: Anything else?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How do I repair pipes and the pump?

lines.speaker: Fancy Dan
lines.text: Well, if you use a pipe section on the damaged part you should be able to replace it easily.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Will I need a hammer?

lines.speaker: Fancy Dan
lines.text: No, likely you'll just need to whack the old one out and slot the new one in with your hands.

lines.speaker: Fancy Dan
lines.text: Anything else?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How do I repair bridges?

lines.speaker: Fancy Dan
lines.text: Well, we have these handy bridge sections you can use to repair the bridges.

lines.speaker: Fancy Dan
lines.text: The main problem is, while a still will keep working if a bit of it is only damaged, the bridges are completely out of commission if they are even slightly damaged.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Is that going to be a problem? I mean, I can jump across I think.

lines.speaker: Fancy Dan
lines.text: Don't try that! Our lawyers tell us we aren't covered for injuries sustained while jumping over burned bridges.

lines.speaker: Fancy Dan
lines.text: Seriously, just keep off the bridges until they are fixed.

lines.speaker: Fancy Dan
lines.text: Anything else?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where do I get all this stuff anyway?

lines.speaker: Fancy Dan
lines.text: We have a load of items already on a table inside the still. You can also make new ones if you grab some logs.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How does that work?

lines.speaker: Fancy Dan
lines.text: We have a work table set up in the still. If you use the logs in it you can craft up a handy replacement for part of the still.

lines.speaker: Fancy Dan
lines.text: Don't worry about them fitting, the things are held together by nails, replacement parts and soot by now.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well...how sanitary...

lines.speaker: Fancy Dan
lines.text: Anything else?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok, I think I've heard enough.

lines.speaker: Fancy Dan
lines.text: Fine. Glad I could help.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So...how would I go about sabotaging the enemy still?

lines.speaker: Fancy Dan
lines.text: Ok, here's what you need to do.

lines.speaker: Fancy Dan
lines.text: The wood around here is really dry and flammable, so take one of the torches, light it up, and then go and attack the enemy still with it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's it?

lines.speaker: Fancy Dan
lines.text: Most of their still is soaked in 'alcohol' and made from tinder dry wood.

lines.speaker: Fancy Dan
lines.text: Essentially, if it isn't ours, try using a torch on it and see what happens.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok, I get you.

lines.speaker: Fancy Dan
lines.text: Anything else?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what will you give me for helping out?

lines.speaker: Fancy Dan
lines.text: Hahahaha! What a mercenary attitude! You'll do fine here.

lines.speaker: Fancy Dan
lines.text: Well, for each bottle that your team produces, I'll give you one piece of eight.

lines.speaker: Fancy Dan
lines.text: If you manage to set something on fire then I'll toss in another, same for repairing the still.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What can I use these pieces of eight for?

lines.speaker: Fancy Dan
lines.text: See Honest Jimmy there? He'll trade you some pretty nice stuff for them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Great!

lines.speaker: Fancy Dan
lines.text: Anything else?
